document.source_title: US plans to shrink forces available to NATO during crises, sources say
document.summary: The Trump administration is set to inform NATO allies about a reduction in U.S. military capabilities available for European defense during crises. This decision reflects a shift in responsibility towards European nations for their own security. The Pentagon plans to announce these changes at a meeting in Brussels, indicating a significant scaling back of U.S. commitments under the NATO Force Model, which could heighten concerns among European allies about U.S. support.
key_takeaways: U.S. plans to reduce military capabilities available to NATO during crises.
The announcement will be made at a meeting of defense policy chiefs in Brussels.
European countries are expected to take on more responsibility for their own security.
Concerns are rising among European allies about U.S. commitment to NATO.
The Pentagon has already announced cuts of 5,000 troops from Europe.
The NATO alliance is experiencing unprecedented strain amid these changes.
document.why_it_matters: This shift in U.S. military strategy could significantly impact NATO's collective defense posture and European security dynamics, raising questions about the future of transatlantic relations and the effectiveness of NATO in responding to crises.

claims.claim_text: The U.S. will shrink the pool of military capabilities available to NATO.
claims.evidence_text: The Trump administration is planning to tell NATO allies this week that it will shrink the pool of military capabilities that the U.S. would have available to assist the alliance’s European nations in a major crisis.

claims.claim_text: European countries are expected to take over primary responsibility for their own security.
claims.evidence_text: U.S. President Donald Trump has made clear he expects European countries to take over primary responsibility for the continent’s security from the United States.

claims.claim_text: The Pentagon plans to announce its intention to lessen its commitment at a meeting in Brussels.
claims.evidence_text: The sources said, however, that the Pentagon plans to announce its intention to lessen its commitment at a Friday meeting of defense policy chiefs in Brussels.

entities.name: NATO
entities.description: The North Atlantic Treaty Organization, a military alliance of European and North American countries.
entities.evidence_text: Under a framework known as the NATO Force Model, the alliance’s member countries identify a pool of available forces that could be activated during a conflict or any other major crisis.

entities.name: Donald Trump
entities.description: Former President of the United States who has influenced U.S. military policy regarding NATO.
entities.evidence_text: U.S. President Donald Trump has made clear he expects European countries to take over primary responsibility for the continent’s security from the United States.

entities.name: Elbridge Colby
entities.description: Pentagon policy chief involved in NATO strategy.
entities.evidence_text: Pentagon policy chief Elbridge Colby has said publicly that the United States will continue to use its nuclear weapons to protect NATO members.

events.event: Announcement of U.S. military capability reduction to NATO
events.evidence_text: The Pentagon plans to announce its intention to lessen its commitment at a Friday meeting of defense policy chiefs in Brussels.
Adjusting the NATO Force Model has emerged as a key priority of Colby’s team heading into the next NATO leaders’ summit, which will take place in Turkey in July.

metrics.name: U.S. troop reduction
metrics.evidence_text: In the past few weeks, the Trump administration has announced plans to cut some 5,000 U.S. troops from Europe.

relationships.subject: U.S.
relationships.evidence_text: A major adjustment to the forces the U.S. would make available during wartime will only intensify those concerns.

citations.title: US plans to shrink forces available to NATO during crises, sources say

source_credibility.reason: The information is based on sources familiar with the matter, indicating a level of insider knowledge but lacking official confirmation.
